title: Between Two Castles of Mad King Ludwig

metadata.notes: Content overhaul — Wingspan teaching standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Room Tiles (147 total)
- **Living rooms** (purple) — score based on specific room types in the 8 surrounding tiles (orthogonal + diagonal). Max 8 VP per tile.
- **Sleeping rooms** (blue) — score 4 VP each if the castle contains at least one of every other room type; only 1 VP each if incomplete.
- **Food rooms** (yellow) — score 2 VP per room of a specified type in specific adjacent positions (above, below, or beside as printed).
- **Outdoor rooms** (green) — score 1 VP per room of a specified type anywhere in the entire castle. Nothing may be built above an outdoor room.
- **Corridor rooms** (white) — score 1 VP per surrounding room (up to 8) bearing the specific wall hanging symbol shown on the tile.
- **Utility rooms** (orange) — score points for connected/chained rooms of specified types. No maximum cap — highest ceiling of any room type.
- **Downstairs rooms** (black) — score 1-2 VP per room of a specified type in the vertical column directly above the downstairs room. Must be placed below the throne room row.
- Each tile shows its name, room type (color-coded), and specific scoring condition.

#### Specialty Rooms & Throne Rooms
- Specialty rooms have unique scoring printed on them — shuffled into the general supply.
- 3 specialty room types earned through bonuses: **Tower** (scores for rooms below it in its column), **Fountain**, and **Grand Foyer**.
- 1 **throne room tile per castle** — the starting centerpiece. Shows 2 desired room types in specific positions, scoring 2 VP per match. Scores 0 VP on its own.

#### Bonus Cards
- 20 bonus cards (28 in Essential Edition) — drawn between rounds.
- Each card adds an end-game scoring condition to one castle (e.g., "3 VP per set of 3 different room types").
- Bonus cards are public information once assigned.

#### Other Components
- Player aids (1 per player) summarizing room types and scoring.
- Score tracking pad for final scoring.
- King token — determines first player and draft direction.
- Rulebook with illustrated placement examples.

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Semi-Cooperative Partnership Building
Every player simultaneously builds **two castles** — one shared with the player on your left, one shared with the player on your right. Your final score is the **LOWER** of your two castles' scores. You must invest in both equally.

#### Game Flow
The game plays over 2 rounds of tile drafting with a bonus phase in between:

**Round 1 — Draft Room Tiles**
1. Each player draws a hand of **9 room tiles** from a face-down supply.
2. Simultaneously, every player selects 2 tiles from their hand (kept secret until all players are ready).
3. Reveal tiles. Place 1 tile into your left castle and 1 into your right castle, discussing placement with each partner.
4. Pass remaining hand to the **left**.
5. Repeat for 4 total turns. After the 4th selection, 1 tile remains — discard it face-down.

**Bonus Phase (Between Rounds)**
1. Each player draws bonus cards (count varies by player count).
2. Assign 1 bonus card to your left castle, 1 to your right castle. Discard extras.
3. Bonus cards provide end-game scoring tied to that specific castle.

**Round 2 — Draft Again**
1. Draw a new hand of 9 tiles.
2. Same drafting process, but pass hands to the **right** (reversed direction).
3. Continue for 4 turns, discard the final remaining tile.

#### The Partnership Dynamic
When placing a tile, discuss with the partner who shares that castle. Both of you want it to score well, but each of you has a second castle competing for attention. Negotiation, compromise, and reading your partners' priorities are essential.

#### 3rd and 5th Tile Bonuses
When the **3rd room of any single type** is placed in a castle, an immediate bonus triggers — extra tiles, specialty rooms, bonus cards, or royal attendants (depending on room type). When the **5th room of a type** is placed, you choose 1 specialty room (Tower, Fountain, or Grand Foyer) to place immediately. These bonuses trigger only once per room type per castle.

#### Building Rules
- All rooms must connect orthogonally to existing tiles, building outward from the throne room.
- The castle grows on a grid — tiles go above, below, left, or right.
- No maximum castle size, but tile supply limits growth.
- Once placed, tiles cannot be moved.
- Sleeping rooms go above the throne room row. Downstairs rooms go below. Nothing goes above outdoor rooms.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Setup
1. Place one throne room tile between each pair of adjacent players (5-player game = 5 castles, arranged in a circle).
2. Shuffle all room tiles face-down into a supply.
3. Shuffle bonus cards into a face-down deck.
4. Give the King token to a random starting player.
5. Each player receives a player aid.

#### Drafting Procedure
- Hand size is always **9 tiles** regardless of player count.
- Each turn: pick 2 tiles secretly, reveal simultaneously, place 1 in each castle, pass remaining hand.
- Round 1 passes **left**. Round 2 passes **right**.
- After 4 turns, the 1 remaining tile is discarded face-down.

#### Placement Rules
- Every tile must be placed orthogonally adjacent to at least one existing tile.
- Discuss placement with your castle partner — it's a shared decision.
- **Downstairs rooms** must go below the throne room row.
- **Sleeping rooms** must go above the throne room row.
- **Outdoor rooms** can go anywhere, but nothing may ever be placed above them.
- Once placed, tiles cannot be moved.

#### Room Type Scoring

**Living Rooms (Purple)** — Score 1 VP per room of a specified type in the 8 surrounding tiles (orthogonal + diagonal). Maximum 8 VP per tile.

**Sleeping Rooms (Blue)** — Score **4 VP each** if the castle has at least one of each of the other 6 room types. Only **1 VP each** if any type is missing. Specialty rooms and the throne room do NOT count toward the 6-type requirement.

**Food Rooms (Yellow)** — Score 2 VP per room of a specified type in specific adjacent positions (directly above, below, or beside as indicated on the tile).

**Outdoor Rooms (Green)** — Score 1 VP per room of a specified type anywhere in the entire castle. Strong scaling but constrain vertical building.

**Corridor Rooms (White)** — Score 1 VP per room in the 8 surrounding tiles that bears a specific wall hanging symbol shown on the corridor tile.

**Utility Rooms (Orange)** — Score points for connected/chained rooms of specified types. No maximum cap — the highest-ceiling room type in the game.

**Downstairs Rooms (Black)** — Score 1 or 2 VP (as printed) per room of a specified type in the vertical column directly above the downstairs room.

#### Bonus Phase
- Draw bonus cards between Round 1 and Round 2.
- Assign 1 card to each of your castles. Discard extras.
- Cards are public — all players can see each castle's bonuses.

#### 2-Player Variant (Ludwig)
A dummy third castle "Ludwig" is placed between the two players, creating 3 castles total. Each player shares one castle with the opponent and one with Ludwig. The adjacent human player makes all placement decisions for the Ludwig side. Your score remains the lower of your two castles.

#### Specialty Rooms
- **Fountains** are specialty rooms, NOT outdoor rooms — they lack the tree icon. Confirmed in official FAQ.
- **Towers** score for the total number of rooms below them in their column.
- **Grand Foyers** score as printed on the tile.

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
The game ends after Round 2 drafting is complete. Every game plays exactly 2 rounds plus the bonus phase — no variable end condition.

#### Castle Scoring
Score each castle individually by summing:

1. **Room tile points** — Evaluate every room tile's scoring condition based on its final position and adjacencies.
2. **Bonus card points** — Evaluate each castle's bonus card(s) based on the castle's final state.
3. **Room type set bonus** — For each complete set of all 7 room types present in a castle, score bonus VP.
4. **Throne room points** — 2 VP per matching room in the correct adjacent position.
5. **Specialty room points** — Towers, Fountains, and Grand Foyers score according to their printed conditions.
6. **Royal Attendant points** — Each scores 1 VP per wall hanging of its specified type anywhere in the castle.

#### Determining Your Score
- Each player contributed to exactly 2 castles.
- Your personal score equals the **LOWER** of your two castles' scores.
- A 70/50 split (score: 50) loses to a 60/60 split (score: 60). Balance is everything.

#### Winning
- Highest personal score wins.
- **Tiebreaker**: Player whose HIGHER castle scored more wins.
- If still tied: shared victory.

#### Typical Score Ranges
- Beginner games: 40-55 VP per castle
- Experienced play: 55-75 VP per castle
- Highly optimized: 75+ VP per castle

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### The Golden Rule: Balance Your Two Castles
Your score is the LOWER of your two castles. A lopsided 80/40 split gives you 40 points, while a balanced 60/60 gives you 60. Never neglect either castle. If one castle is pulling ahead, invest your next tile in the weaker one.

#### Room Type Priorities
- **Utility rooms scale best.** A utility room scoring "1 VP per food room in this castle" grows with every food room added. Place utility rooms early and build around their condition. No scoring cap means they can be the highest-scoring individual tiles in the game.
- **Sleeping rooms are high-risk, high-reward.** At 4 VP each with all 6 other types present vs. 1 VP without — they can swing 15+ points. Commit early to collecting all types or avoid sleeping rooms entirely.
- **Outdoor rooms want edges.** They score for rooms across the entire castle (strong scaling), but nothing can be built above them. Place them at the top of columns or castle edges.
- **Downstairs rooms go below.** They score for rooms in the column directly above them — not the whole castle. Place them deep below the throne room with tall columns above.

#### Pursue 3rd-Tile Bonuses
When the 3rd room of any type is placed, a bonus triggers — extra tiles, specialty rooms, bonus cards, or royal attendants. These bonuses are often worth more than any single tile's raw scoring. Reaching 3 of a type should be a deliberate goal, not an accident.

#### Communicate With Your Partners
You share each castle with a different player. Talk about what you need. If your left partner is filling your shared castle with sleeping rooms, tell your right partner you need food and outdoor rooms for the set bonus. Collaboration beats silent play.

#### Hate-Drafting
In a drafting game, denying an opponent a key tile that would complete their set or trigger a bonus can be as valuable as taking a good tile for yourself. Watch what your neighbors are building.

#### Common Beginner Mistakes
- **Neglecting one castle** — Your weaker castle IS your score. Check both castles every turn.
- **Ignoring adjacency** — Many rooms score based on what's next to them. Random placement wastes points.
- **Hoarding one room type** — 6 sleeping rooms but no food or outdoor rooms = missed set bonuses and poor diversity.
- **Burying outdoor rooms** — They score for open edges and constrain building above. Place them on top, not in the middle.
- **Not reading bonus cards** — Bonus cards can be worth 10+ VP if built around. Assign them to the castle that best fits.
- **Fighting your partner** — A slightly suboptimal placement in a cooperative partnership beats a perfect placement that makes your partner abandon your castle.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Between Two Castles

**Setup**: Each pair of adjacent players shares a castle with a throne room tile in the center. You have two castles — one to your left, one to your right. Take your player aid and study the room type summary.

**Round 1, Turn 1**: Draw 9 room tiles. Look at all of them. Find 2 tiles that score well together or start a plan — ideally one for each castle. Pick 2 tiles, keep them secret. When everyone reveals: place 1 tile in your left castle, 1 in your right castle. Discuss with each partner where the tile should go. Remember: downstairs rooms go below the throne row, sleeping rooms go above. Pass your remaining 7 tiles to the left.

**Round 1, Turn 2**: You receive 7 tiles from the player on your right. Scan for tiles that complement what you've already placed. A utility room that scores "1 VP per food room" pairs perfectly with a castle that already has food rooms. Pick 2, reveal, place, pass left.

**Round 1, Turns 3-4**: Hands shrink to 5 then 3 tiles. Fewer choices mean you must adapt. After Turn 4, discard the 1 remaining tile. Check both castles — are they roughly balanced? Does either need a specific room type for the sleeping room set bonus?

**Bonus Phase**: Draw bonus cards. Read each carefully. Assign the card that best fits your left castle's existing layout to the left, and the other to the right. These are public — your partners can see them too.

**Round 2**: Draw a fresh hand of 9 tiles. Same process, but pass to the **right** this time. Focus on completing room type sets (for the sleeping room 4 VP bonus) and building around your bonus cards. Try to trigger 3rd-tile bonuses by getting a 3rd room of any type.

**Scoring**: Score each castle tile by tile. Sum room tile points + bonus card points + set bonuses + throne room bonuses + specialty room points. Your personal score = the LOWER castle. First game target: 50-60 per castle is solid. 65+ is excellent.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Castle Balance is Everything

Like Between Two Cities, your score is your LOWER castle. Never let one castle fall more than 10 points behind the other. If your left castle is struggling, prioritize it even if your right castle could score more.

#### Room Synergy Chains

Rooms score based on adjacency and type. **Food rooms** (yellow) want to be near Sleeping rooms. **Activity rooms** (red) score for unique types nearby. **Living rooms** (orange) want size. **Utility rooms** (gray) are flexible connectors. **Outdoor rooms** (green) score for being on ground level. Build clusters, not scattered rooms.

#### Throne Room Placement

Each castle has a throne room that scores for specific conditions. Identify these conditions during setup and draft rooms that satisfy them. A 10-point throne room bonus often decides the game.

#### Draft Signaling

In a 2-player collaboration, communication is limited to your drafting choices. If your partner passes you food rooms, they're signaling the castle needs food rooms. Respond by continuing that strategy. Fighting your partner's signals hurts both of you.

#### Basement and Towers

Basement rooms (below ground) and towers (above 2nd floor) are often overlooked but provide unique scoring opportunities. Basements are especially powerful — fewer players compete for them.

rulings: Fountains are specialty rooms, NOT outdoor rooms — they lack the tree icon that identifies outdoor rooms.

Sleeping rooms require at least one of each of the 6 other regular room types (food, living, utility, outdoor, corridor, downstairs) to score 4 VP. Specialty rooms and the throne room do NOT count toward this requirement.
Downstairs rooms score for the vertical column directly above the downstairs room — not the entire castle.
Nothing may ever be placed above an outdoor room tile. This is a hard placement constraint.
Each player always draws 9 tiles per round regardless of player count.
When the 3rd room of any type is placed in a castle, an immediate bonus triggers. The 5th room of a type lets players choose one specialty room (Tower, Fountain, or Grand Foyer) to place.
Bonus cards are public information — all players can see which bonuses each castle has after assignment.
In the 2-player Ludwig variant, the adjacent human player makes all placement decisions for the Ludwig side.
